reasoning: The qwen model's 'unsafe' verdict stems from a misinterpretation of PKGBUILD execution order. The pkgver() function runs during makepkg --printsrcinfo, which doesn't require source download/extraction. The actual binary execution only occurs during full makepkg after checksum verification.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly assumed pkgver() executes downloaded binaries before verification, while deepseek properly understood PKGBUILD execution flow and context.
coverage_issues: qwen failed to review critical files: .install script and runtime configs were ignored after stopping at PKGBUILD
Deepseek wasted resources reviewing irrelevant application files (ftplugins, lua modules) not executed during build/install
false_positives: qwen: PKGBUILD pkgver() execution risk - misinterpreted when binaries are actually executed (only after verification during full build)
learnings: Prioritize PKGBUILD/.install scripts over application code
Train models on makepkg execution phases: --printsrcinfo doesn't download/execute sources
Flag irrelevant file selections (editor configs/runtime files) as low priority
Improve detection of build-stage context: code in src/ is irrelevant unless executed by PKGBUILD
